title: Run a Mock Campaign
description: Plan and execute a simulated political campaign from scratch — candidate positioning, messaging, voter targeting, fundraising plan, field operations strategy, and media plan. Present your campaign plan to a panel for critique.
whyItMatters: Running a campaign requires integrating every skill in this curriculum — strategy, communications, operations, and leadership. This project forces you to make the hard trade-offs that real campaigns face: limited money, limited time, and unlimited demands.

steps.title: Set the scenario
steps.description: Choose a real electorate and a realistic scenario: you are a first-time candidate running for the opposition party in a marginal seat.
steps.tips: Use real data from your electorate mapping project if you completed it.

steps.title: Build the candidate profile
steps.description: Create a complete candidate profile: biography, values, policy positions on 10 key issues, personal strengths and vulnerabilities.
steps.tips: Include a credible weakness — no candidate is perfect. The plan should address how to manage it.

steps.title: Develop the campaign strategy
steps.description: Write a 5-page campaign strategy: target voter segments, core message, three key policy platforms, positioning against the opponent.
steps.tips: The strategy should fit on five pages. If it takes more, you haven't made your choices yet.

steps.title: Create the operations plan
steps.description: Design the field operation: volunteer targets, door-knocking schedule, phone bank plan, election day logistics. Include a weekly activity calendar for 8 weeks.
steps.tips: Be realistic about volunteer numbers. Overestimating volunteers is the most common campaign planning mistake.

steps.title: Build the communications plan
steps.description: Create: a press release announcing candidacy, three social media posts, a one-minute radio ad script, and a crisis response plan for one anticipated attack.
steps.tips: Every piece of content should reinforce the same core message.

steps.title: Present and defend
steps.description: Present your campaign plan in a 20-minute presentation. Prepare for 15 minutes of questions from sceptical panellists.
steps.tips: Anticipate the hardest question: 'Why will this campaign lose?' If you can answer that honestly, you have a real plan.

deliverables: An operations plan with weekly calendar
A communications kit (press release, social posts, radio script, crisis plan)

rubric.description: Does every element of the campaign — message, targeting, operations — align with a coherent strategy?
Is the plan achievable with realistic volunteer, funding, and time constraints?
Does the core message come through in every piece of communications material?
Does the plan anticipate and prepare for likely attacks and setbacks?
Can the candidate present and defend the plan under questioning?
